2606.00156.title: A physics-informed foundation model for quantitative diffusion MRI
2606.00156.paper_summary: Understanding the human brain requires access to its microscopic tissue architecture. Diffusion magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) provides the only noninvasive window into whole-brain microstructure in vivo, yet reliable quantitative mapping remains confined to specialized research settings requiring dense sampling and optimized acquisition protocols. To address this gap, we present a physics-informed generative microstructure network (PIGMENT) that learns a universal generative prior of human brain microstructure and adapts it zero-shot to each participant's measured data to recover subject-specific maps. Trained on 11375 scans spanning multiple sites, vendors, and field strengths, PIGMENT enabled reliable quantitative mapping for tensor, kurtosis, and NODDI models across external datasets from five independent centers. It remains effective where conventional fitting becomes unreliable, recovering meaningful maps from extremely sparse acquisitions while supporting downstream tractography and structural connectivity mapping. PIGMENT estimates demonstrated strong biological validity, preserving submillimeter cortical microarchitectural patterns and early-childhood white matter developmental trajectories from 10-fold accelerated scans. Furthermore, PIGMENT enables reliable quantitative tensor mapping on cost-efficient low-field systems and the extraction of tumor-related biomarkers using ultra-fast clinical protocols. Together, these results establish PIGMENT as a physics-informed foundation model that extends quantitative diffusion MRI into regimes traditionally too sparse, heterogeneous, or clinically constrained for reliable analysis.
2606.00156.paper_summary_zh: 理解人腦需要接觸其微觀組織結構。擴散磁共振成像（MRI）提供了唯一的非侵入性窗口，能夠在活體中觀察整個大腦的微結構，但可靠的定量映射仍然局限於需要密集採樣和優化獲取協議的專門研究環境。為了解決這一差距，我們提出了一種物理知識驅動的生成微結構網絡（PIGMENT），它學習人腦微結構的通用生成先驗，並將其零樣本適應到每個參與者的測量數據，以恢復特定於受試者的地圖。PIGMENT在跨多個地點、供應商和場強的11375個掃描上進行訓練，使得在來自五個獨立中心的外部數據集中，能夠可靠地進行張量、峰度和NODDI模型的定量映射。它在傳統擬合變得不可靠的情況下仍然有效，從極度稀疏的獲取中恢復有意義的地圖，同時支持下游的纖維追蹤和結構連接映射。PIGMENT的估計顯示出強大的生物有效性，保留了亞毫米皮質微結構模式和早期兒童白質發展軌跡，這些都是來自10倍加速掃描的數據。此外，PIGMENT還能在成本效益高的低場系統上進行可靠的定量張量映射，並使用超快速臨床協議提取與腫瘤相關的生物標記。綜合這些結果，PIGMENT建立了一個物理知識驅動的基礎模型，將定量擴散MRI擴展到傳統上過於稀疏、異質或臨床受限的領域，以便進行可靠的分析。
2606.00156.author: Zihan Li et.al.
2606.00156.authors: Zihan Li, Jialan Zheng, Ziyu Li, Xun Yuan, Kasidit Anmahapong, Ziang Wang, Mingxuan Liu, Hongjia Yang, Yifei Chen, Zhuhao Wang, Yuhang He, Fang Chen, Rui Li, Huaiqiang Sun, Yi Liao, Congyu Liao, Yang Yang, Haibo Qu, Xue Zhang, Hongen Liao, Qiyuan Tian
